title: Release v9.2.55 — Help Center Impact
summary: Release v9.2.55 ships 14 changes across six product areas. Customer-visible work concentrates in PM Operations (expense file sharing now auto-detects owners), Accounting / Reporting v2 (running balance column returns to Transaction Details; insurance status chip now shows Expired and Future), Tenant Experience & Payments (new Rent Payments Overview with an Additional Payments page and an in-app notification announcement), Leads & Leasing (showings dialogs no longer break when availability isn't set), Workflows (Realtor.com listing now visible with a New tag), and Onboarding & Imports (cancelled accounts can re-subscribe at their previous unit count). The remaining items are backend reliability and telemetry — no Help Center change needed.

title: Release v9.2.59 — Help Center Impact
summary: Release v9.2.59 ships 3 changes — two are improvements to the Voice module in the Communications Center (still behind the Voice beta flag): smarter recipient experience when a caller drops mid-screening, and AI-generated suggested-action chips that appear under each call summary so a single tap creates a contact, sends a rental application, or files a task. The third item is a backend reliability fix to the SSO login redirect that eliminates a class of 500 errors. No general-availability features in this release that need Help Center coverage today; the Voice module additions are candidates for a refreshed Voice / AI-summary article once GA.

title: Release v9.2.62 — Help Center Impact
summary: Release v9.2.62 ships 2 internal-only changes — a performance fix to the new Get Started V2 wizard so editing one field in a 50-unit setup no longer re-renders the whole tree, and Phase 1 of decoupling RabbitMQ topology assertion from the server into a standalone library + Lambda + CLI. No customer-facing changes; no Help Center updates needed.

title: Release v9.2.63 — Help Center Impact
summary: Release v9.2.63 ships 14 changes across PM Operations, Onboarding & Imports, Tenant Experience & Payments, and Leads & Leasing. The headline customer-impact item is Showings is now restricted to Pro and Premium plans — Starter customers will no longer see the showings nav, screens, prospect cards, or comms-center action (L2L-1141). Other customer-facing work: the AI Communications Center auto-responder ships a real settings UI with a first-time dialog and AI-add-on paywall (PMO-1010); the AI tenants resolution feature gets re-platformed as a new Concierge Agent card under Settings → AI Agents (PMO-602); income verification gets a new adoption popup at the end of the unit-listing wizard (L2L-890) and a friendlier translation pass; the rent-payments overview now carries a dismissible "share feedback" banner (TEN-368); and a couple of bug-fix polish items hit work-order email status labels (PMO-998) and property-import owner matching (ACT-412). The remaining 7 items are internal refactors, server-side APIs, schema validation, telemetry, and tracking work — no Help Center impact.

title: Release v9.2.66 — Help Center Impact
summary: Release v9.2.66 ships 10 changes across PM Operations, Accounting, Onboarding & Imports, Tenant Experience & Payments, Leads & Leasing, and Banking. Customer-facing work is mostly an extension of items already in flight: PMO-988 builds on last week's expense-file owner auto-detection (PMO-605, v9.2.55) by adding a multi-select dropdown, a "set as default" option, and a paperclip-icon refresh on the Owner Portal files page; the Additional Payments screen — broken since launch — now correctly shows alternative payment methods (TEN-397); and a second adoption nudge for Income Verification lands on the Rental Applications screen via a "What's New" dialog. ACT-598 polishes visual inconsistencies in the login/signup fields, including a placeholder that was covering the password value. Everything else is internal: a Renewals Agent API endpoint, a small GSF V2 redirect fix, two Bugsnag noise-reduction tasks, an env-detection refinement, and a Slack alert routing fix.

title: Release v9.2.67 — Help Center Impact
summary: Release v9.2.67 ships 4 changes. One customer-visible polish item: clearer copy on the 2FA "remember this device for 30 days" checkbox (ACT-649). One Voice-module improvement still behind the beta flag — inactive teammates now show an Inactive tag in voice settings and are skipped during call forwarding (PMO-1004). Two internal items: a Mongo write-conflict retry on the BATCH_IMPORT sync paths (ACT-398), and a backend chart-of-accounts routing fix for payments/payouts (unlinked).

title: Release v9.2.71 — Help Center Impact
summary: Release v9.2.71 ships 26 changes across seven product areas, with the heaviest investment in PM Operations voice features (9 items) and a multi-line rental-application fee overhaul in Leads & Leasing. Customer-visible highlights include MMS support and unknown-contact auto-assignment in the Communications Center, emergency-contact email on the tenant overview, and a Get Started fix that stops migrated leases from showing phantom security-deposit balances. Most of the voice routing, holding-fee, and banking-notification work is still behind feature flags (beta), and roughly a third of the release is internal NFR/infrastructure hardening with no Help Center impact.

title: Release v9.2.75 — Help Center Impact
summary: Release v9.2.75 is a small release of 4 changes. The one customer-facing highlight is self-serve payment dispute management in Banking (BAN-348), which lets property managers review, draft, and submit evidence for Stripe payment disputes directly inside DoorLoop instead of routing through the risk team. The remaining three items are internal hardening: a new background queue that makes voice post-call transcription/summary more reliable, scaffolding for a new communications-purchases library, and a rent-reminder performance optimization. Only the dispute feature has Help Center impact — and it's a content gap (no existing article covers disputes).

title: Release v9.2.76 — Help Center Impact
summary: This release ships 22 changes across 7 areas. Customer-visible highlights include a redesigned vendor portal with messaging and file uploads (PMO-221), AI Communications Center guided setup and escalation notifications (PMO-1493), rental applications in global search (WOR-822), and corrected transfer notification wording (BAN-789). Three features are in beta behind feature flags: late-balance-notice workflows (WOR-430/432) and a new rental application decision dialog (L2L-1151). The remaining 11 items are internal infrastructure, monitoring, and performance fixes.

title: Release v9.2.79 — Help Center Impact
summary: This release ships 7 changes across PM Operations (3), Onboarding & Imports (3), and Tenant Experience & Payments (1). The customer-visible highlights are: vendor inactive status with toggles and list filtering (PMO-986), portfolio filtering in Communications Center (PMO-1462), enhanced Communications Center UI with tenant presence indicators and auto-responder controls (PMO-641), and localized SSO invitation error messages for the tenant portal (ACT-612). Three items are internal infrastructure with no Help Center impact.

title: Release v9.2.80 — Help Center Impact
summary: This release ships 4 changes across PM Operations (3) and Leads & Leasing (1). The headline customer-facing feature adds a Service Location callout on work order details and printouts for units with distinct addresses — helping vendors arrive at the right location. A SEV-2 fix restores the rental applications listings page for companies whose units have no rent set. Two internal fixes resolve a global search note-click crash and suppress voice call error log noise.

title: Release v9.2.83 — Help Center Impact
summary: This release ships 3 changes across PM Operations (1), Tenant Experience & Payments (1), and Banking (1). All three are internal infrastructure or backend changes: inspection eligibility tracking for analytics, RabbitMQ pipeline wiring for DoorWatch risk incidents, and a Stripe webhook handler to keep payout bank account data in sync. No customer-facing UI changes require Help Center updates.

title: Release v9.2.85 — Help Center Impact
summary: This release ships 10 changes across PM Operations (1), Accounting (1), Onboarding & Imports (3), Tenant Experience & Payments (1), Workflows (2), Leads & Leasing (1), and Other (1). The two headline customer-facing items are offline-first inspection editing on mobile (PMO-1570, behind a feature flag) and saved views + CSV/Excel export for property/unit lists in Accounting. Workflows gains a configurable outstanding-balance threshold for Late Balance Notice triggers (WOR-1090, also flag-gated). The remaining items are internal reliability, monitoring, and infrastructure work.

title: Release v9.2.87 — Help Center Impact
summary: This release ships 8 changes across PM Operations (3), Tenant Experience & Payments (3 internal), Workflows (1 internal), and Leads & Leasing (1). Customer-visible highlights: missed inbound calls to unconfigured DoorLoop numbers are now reliably tracked in voice reports (PMO-1551 bug fix), and the AI auto-responder no longer stamps SMS replies server-side — the AI agent controls the full message text (PMO-1556). UI polish adds New-badge indicators to showing entry points (settings, prospects, invitations). Four items are internal infrastructure.

title: Release v9.2.92 — Help Center Impact
summary: This release ships 4 changes, all in Tenant Experience & Payments. Two are customer-facing: a first-time card payment discount A/B experiment for eligible one-time payers in the tenant portal (TEN-467, experiment-gated), and tenants can now mark notifications as read with instant badge count updates (TEN-490). Two are internal: invitation pageview analytics moved server-side for full-coverage tracking (TEN-522), and MongoDB models for tasks/rental applications/eSignature/policy/budget migrated to the shared library (TEN-243).

title: Releases v9.2.95 + v9.2.96 — Help Center Impact
summary: Two releases shipped June 11, 2026 (v9.2.95 and v9.2.96) covering 11 tickets. Customer-visible highlights: improved Stripe billing error messages in English and Spanish (ACT-750), tenant notification preferences now saving and syncing across mobile and web (TEN-494), two new v2 Reports Home entries for Tasks and Work Orders (DOO-2477, DOO-2478 — non-final), OTP login 500 error fixed (ACT-769), and Open Bills routing to the v2 AP Aging Summary (TIG-2608). Four tickets are internal infrastructure; one (L2L-1156) is behind a feature flag.

title: Release v9.2.97 — Help Center Impact
summary: Release v9.2.97 ships 4 changes across Accounting, Tenant Experience & Payments, Workflows, and Other. Transaction list reports now show 'Memo' instead of 'Description' as the column header (DOO-1507). The Rent Payments Overview dashboard receives a QA fix pass for average collection time and overdue messaging (TEN-396). The Outstanding Charges Table chip for Late Balance Notice emails (WOR-1106) is still In Review. Unpaid Rent now routes directly to the v2 AR Aging Summary for users on the new reporting engine (TIG-2590).

title: Release v9.2.99 — Help Center Impact
summary: Release v9.2.99 ships one customer-facing change: per-property Welcome SMS customization in the Communication Center (PMO-968). Property managers can now set a unique welcome message for each property with a default fallback, addressing a top upmarket request from large accounts managing multiple owners or LLCs. Three existing SMS and Communication Center articles need updating.

title: Release v9.3.0 — Help Center Impact
summary: Release v9.3.0 ships 16 changes across four product areas. Headlines: lease document tracking with tenant portal sharing (PMO-203), international 2FA SMS/voice fallback (ACT-744), Lemonade renters insurance in 5 new states (TEN-579), and a redesigned Pay-or-Quit workflow template with distinct lavender branding (WOR-1123). Owner Portal file search/filtering (PMO-1783) and Late Balance Notice workflow execution (WOR-430) ship behind feature flags. Six items are internal infrastructure with no Help Center impact.

title: Release v9.3.6 — Help Center Impact
summary: Release v9.3.6 ships 7 changes across 5 product areas. The most Help Center–relevant changes are a copy fix in Tenant Assistant Settings (PM Operations) and early duplicate-validation on batch tenant imports (Onboarding & Imports). The remaining 5 items are internal analytics instrumentation, a Salesforce API optimization, and a Leads & Leasing UI framework upgrade — none requiring immediate article updates.
